title: Hadrolaelia pygmaea
intro: Hadrolaelia pygmaea, also known as the Pygmy Hadrolaelia, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Brazil. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and infections, due to its reported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Hadrolaelia pygmaea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Hadrolaelia pygmaea is a species of orchid native to the Americas. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. At the genus level, it is classified under the subtribe Pleurothallidinae.
